[{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​What’s actually happening with AI right now? We’re not here for noise - we’re here for what’s real.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Come join us (the NayaOne community) for our next Enterprise AI meetup! ", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "The banks pulling ahead on AI aren't waiting for one platform to do everything. They're assembling a stack, layer by layer, of focused tools that each earn their place by doing one thing brilliantly, inside the rules.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Data extraction that's fully traceable. Decision-making that's fully explainable. No black boxes. No hallucinations. Every output auditable, every conclusion defensible. That's what it takes to get AI out of the lab and into live operations in a regulated environment.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "It's happening now. And the stack is starting to look the same across the institutions that are furthest ahead.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This event shows what that stack looks like with two live demos:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "TextMine", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " - Financial institutions have been sitting on mountains of unstructured documents for decades. TextMine is changing that. Their AI extracts clean, structured data from contracts, filings, and KYC packs in minutes - fully traceable, audit-ready, and already replacing processes that haven't evolved since 2005.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Rainbird", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " - AI that is Deterministic. Explainable. Every decision backed by a complete evidence chain. Rainbird is rewriting the rules on what AI can be trusted to do in regulated environments - already live across underwriting, claims, and suitability at major financial institutions.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Plus pizza and beer as always!", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "It’s first come, first served. If your job touches AI in any way, you probably want to be in this room.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}]}, {"type": "horizontal_rule"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "About NayaOne Community Events", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We bring together the people building, buying, challenging, and shipping financial technology - to share what’s actually working, what’s hard, and what’s next.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "horizontal_rule"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "NayaOne", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://nayaone.com/"}}]}, {"text": " is the Vendor Evaluation Infrastructure that transforms how financial institutions & other regulated industries adopt new technology. We provide a unified, secure platform to rapidly discover, validate, and prepare vendors for onboarding, cutting the time-to-market from months to just weeks with auditable evidence.", "type": "text"}]}]

"https://x.com/vivek_veeriah"}}]}, {"text": ", Research Scientist at Google DeepMind: ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Generating Creative Chess Puzzles", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/aaronbenjaminjones/?originalSubdomain=uk"}}]}, {"text": "Gabriela Tropia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.arts.ac.uk/colleges/central-saint-martins/people/gabriela-tropia"}}]}, {"text": ", Artist-Filmmaker and Educator, ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "AI, film and digital ​necromancy", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​​​​​19:50 Talks finish, networking with drinks & food", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​​​​20:30 Event close", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​We are grateful to Adobe for hosting & sponsoring the event.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​​​—", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​​​​More on talks and speakers:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Aaron Hertzmann", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.dgp.toronto.edu/~hertzman/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", Principal Scientist at Adobe Research: Per-Fixation Perspective in Art and Photography", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Conventional theories of pictures based on linear perspective projection fail to explain the kinds of pictures that we actually make and see in the world. I propose a new approach to understanding how human vision interprets 3D shape in realistic pictures, specifically focusing on perspective. This theory combines ideas from art history, human vision, and computational photography, and gives new insights into the use of perspective in painting, photography, and digital imagery.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Aaron Hertzmann", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.dgp.toronto.edu/~hertzman/"}}]}, {"text": " is a Principal Scientist at Adobe Research, and Affiliate Faculty at the University of Washington. He received a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science and Art at Rice University and a PhD degree in Computer Science from New York University. He was previously a Professor of Computer Science at the University of Toronto for ten years. He has published over 125 papers in computer graphics, the science of art, and AI. He is an IEEE Fellow, an ACM Fellow, and winner of the 2024 SIGGRAPH Computer Graphics Achievement Award. His research led to a Technical Emmy Award won by Adobe in 2025. ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Vivek Veeriah", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://x.com/vivek_veeriah"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", Research Scientist at Google DeepMind: Generating Creative Chess Puzzles", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "While Generative AI rapidly advances in various domains, generating truly creative, aesthetic, and counter-intuitive outputs remains a challenge. Looking at chess puzzles, we introduce a reinforcement learning framework with novel rewards based on chess engine search statistics. The rewards are designed to enhance a puzzle's uniqueness, counter-intuitiveness, diversity, and realism. ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Our puzzles", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://sites.google.com/view/puzzle-gen"}}]}, {"text": " meet novelty and diversity benchmarks, retain aesthetic themes, and are rated by human experts as more creative, enjoyable, and counter-intuitive than composed book puzzles, even approaching classic compositions.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Vivek Veeriah", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://x.com/vivek_veeriah"}}]}, {"text": " is a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind, where he builds creative agents that discover new knowledge and continuously improve over time. He has worked on notable projects including AlphaProof, Creative Puzzle Generation, and AlphaZero_db. His research spans diverse fields, including Large Language Models (LLMs), Reinforcement Learning (RL), Reasoning and Synthetic data generation. He holds a PhD in Machine Learning from the University of Michigan.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Gabriela Tropia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.arts.ac.uk/colleges/central-saint-martins/people/gabriela-tropia"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", Artist-Filmmaker and Educator, ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "AI, film and digital ​necromancy", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}, {"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Gabriela Tropia will discuss her artistic practice across dance, film and AI. She will present an extract from her award-winning AI film ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Organising Principles of Experience", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}, {"text": ", in which she digitally resurrects experimental filmmaker Maya Deren and invites this AI model to imagine new films. Drawing on her background in dance, the talk explores choreography and directing performance in AI generated videos, offering insights into embodiment, collaboration and authorship.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Gabi Tropia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.arts.ac.uk/colleges/central-saint-martins/people/gabriela-tropia"}}]}, {"text": " is an artist-filmmaker and educator working across film, performance, and emerging technologies. At Central Saint Martins (UAL), where she leads the MA Performance: Screen, she is developing ways to collaborate with AI and exploring the creative possibilities of AI film as a new medium. She previously founded the first MA Screendance course in the world. Her shorts have been screened at Cinedans Amsterdam, Passion for Freedom, Sadlers Wells and the National Museum of Singapore. ", "type": "text"}]}]

event.name: Talking Tachles - How We Actually Use AI at Work - Israeli Tech Parliament AI Showcase Volume #1

description: [{"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Update: Please bring a photo ID or else you won't be able to pass security (Amazon are rather strict ;) ).", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Everyone is talking about AI. Far fewer people are showing how they actually use it in their day-to-day work.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We’re launching a new Israeli Tech Parliament series where members of the ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "community share practical AI workflows, real use cases, and honest lessons from the tools they actually use in their day jobs.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Each event will feature community speakers, each sharing a concrete use case from their own work, followed by Q&A and some Firgun and networking.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Topics across the series may include coding workflows, AI agents, analytics, personal knowledge systems, dashboards, prompting, context management, and practical lessons from using AI inside real teams and companies.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Expect practical demos, useful takeaways, and a chance to meet others experimenting with similar tools and ideas.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Our speakers:", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Boaz Tal -  Head of Business Development at Bless Data and HumanFirst. He co-founded starkeyNET in 2000, an early NLP and voice recognition company. He is currently developing \"Open Brain\" because he wanted a single memory layer that travels across every AI he uses. It’s working well enough that he’s turning it into a commercial product.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Ran Lekach - Founder of BrainStorm Technologies, where he focuses on improving how people interact with AI to achieve more accurate, reliable, and intuitive outcomes. With more than 20 years of experience across multiple technology domains, he brings a strong background in both innovation and real-world implementation. In recent years, Ran has specialised in artificial intelligence while continuing his work in banking technology, giving him a unique perspective on how AI is reshaping established industries.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "​Ido Kaz is a Product Manager with a deep technical foundation in semiconductor engineering. He specializes in bridging the gap between complex infrastructure and seamless developer experiences across the FinTech, Cybersecurity, and Consumer Tech sectors. With a proven track record in optimizing core internal processes and scaling client-facing products, Ido focuses on building high-performance tools that empower developers and delight users.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Daniel Dubezki, Early Stage Startups Account Manager, HCLS Sector Lead, Amazon Web Services", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Daniel works with early-stage startups across the UK, helping founders navigate their AWS journey. As HCLS sector lead, he focuses on the intersection of life sciences, health tech, and cloud infrastructure.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "At AWS, Daniel has been running a Slack community of 3,000+ AWS field members exploring how AI tools can change the way sellers work day to day. He led internal enablement sessions on Kiro (Amazon’s agentic IDE product) and will share how field teams are using AI to move faster, work smarter, and sell AI products with more confidence.", "type": "text"}]}]

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Canva Code AI Hackathon for Event Professionals and Community Builders", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hosted by ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Eventify", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://eventify.io"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " in collaboration with ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Canva", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://canva.com"}}]}, {"text": " UK and ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Led by Community", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.ledby.community/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Join us in London for a hands-on hackathon designed for event professionals who want to explore how AI can improve how events are planned, delivered, and experienced.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This is a practical working session for people in the events industry. Whether you work in-house, at an agency, or in a corporate events team, this hackathon is designed to help you turn real event challenges into useful AI-powered solutions.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Using ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Canva Code", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", you will collaborate with other event professionals, community builders, operators, marketers, producers, and technical minds to build ideas that support attendee journeys, event operations, networking, speaker support, content workflows, reporting, engagement, and more.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "You must bring your laptop to participate!", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "You do not need to be a developer to take part. calendar.description_short: We advance AI security by bringing together innovators, researchers, builders, and defenders to share real-world experience securing AI-powered and agentic applications.

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "AI Security Summit London — May 14, 2026", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hey everyone, we're partnering with the upcoming AI Security Summit in London. featured_guests.bio_short: A venture builder who wants to build a better human assistant that really works and ethical

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Join David Wood (London Futurists), World renowned futurist Brett King, along with leading expert on AI and the future of oral health, Dr Julia Michelin as we host a lunchtime gathering of London Futurists and friends in the upstairs room at Ye Olde Cock Tavern from 12 noon to 2pm on Thursday 14th May.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Brett co-hosts the only futurists podcast with a cooler name than \"London Futurists Podcast\", namely \"The Futurists\": ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "https://www.thefuturists.com/", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.thefuturists.com/"}}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Dr Julia is one of the world’s leading experts on AI in Dentistry having authored the book The Future of Dentistry and founded LumaBrush, an app-enabled, smart toothbrush with an intra-oral camera that detects a range of oral diseases such as cavities at home  ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Brett, Julia, and David will be catalysing a conversation about (what else?) the future - including the future of finance, the future of health, the future of AI, the future of dentistry, and the future of geo-politics post Iran and AGI.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "A minor complication: if you come to this, you'll need to order your food in advance, picking options from the usual menu, ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/greater-london/ye-olde-cock-tavern/menu", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/greater-london/ye-olde-cock-tavern/menu"}}]}, {"text": ".", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Contact us for more information via WhatsApp (+1-646-204-0492 or +44 7899 803858)", "type": "text"}]}]

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "MCP is changing how developers build with AI, but we're just scratching the surface. Join us for an evening of talks that go beyond retrieval to explore what's actually possible when you give agents real tools, real constraints and real APIs.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Talks", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "MCP: Context is Everything! - Carly Richmond (Developer Advocate Lead, Elastic)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "MCP is a powerful tool for giving LLMs capabilities to not just retrieve information, but to automate key actions based on relevant data. Let’s see how it can be used for retrieving relevant context and other activities such as observability.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Efficient tools with MCP code mode - Confidence Okoghenun (Senior Developer Advocate, Cloudflare)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Traditional MCP approaches choke on context windows: Cloudflare's 2,500+ endpoint API would require 1.17 million tokens. Code Mode flips the script by having LLMs write code against typed APIs instead of making direct tool calls, achieving 99.9% token reduction. Come learn how code mode works and how to optimize your MCP tools with it.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Sandboxes: how to limit agents so we can use them more - Patrick Dawkins (Principal Engineer, Upsun)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We want our agents to run longer and use more tools, but we're hampered by constant prompts for approval. Sandboxes are the practical middle ground: isolate the agent so you can stop watching every command and let it work. This talk covers what \"sandbox\" means, who provides them today and the Linux primitives that let you build one yourself.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Cooking with MCP in VS Code - Liam Hampton (Senior Cloud Advocate, Microsoft)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "In this session Liam will show you how VS Code is fully supporting the MCP spec, from MCP Apps to sandboxing and elicitations.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Agenda", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "6:00 PM - Doors open, registration and networking", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "6:10 PM - Welcome (Upsun & Cloudflare)", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "6:20 PM - MCP: Context is Everything! - Carly Richmond, Elastic ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "6:45 PM - Sandboxes: how to limit agents so we can use them more - Patrick Dawkins, Upsun", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "7:00 PM - Break (Food & Drinks)", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "7:30 PM - Cooking with MCP in VS Code - Liam Hampton, Microsoft ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "7:55 PM - Efficient tools with MCP code mode - Confidence Okoghenun, Cloudflare", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "8:15 PM - Open networking and drinks", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "9:00 PM - Close", "type": "text"}]}]

event.name: AI in Finance: Workshop & Dinner
event.full_address: 7-10 John Adam St, London WC2N 6AA, UK

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Last month I hosted a private finance dinner in London.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We invited 15 CFOs and finance leaders. Some of the sharpest finance minds I've met. Every single one of them pulled me aside at some point during the evening and asked the same question.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "\"Where do I actually start with AI?\"", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Not one of them had a clear answer. Not because they weren't paying attention. Because nobody has given them a real starting point. Just vendor decks, conference panels, and headlines that don't translate into anything actionable.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "That dinner is why I'm hosting this.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "On Thursday the 14th, we're running a hands-on workshop for finance leaders who are done waiting for clarity and ready to build it. Two hours of practical work. Real questions. Real starting points. Then dinner.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "If you're a CFO, Controller, or VP/Director of finance and you're still figuring out where AI fits in your function, this room is for you.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Seats are capped to keep the conversation real and useful.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Come join us. event.name: Reimagining Software Development with AI
event.full_address: AutogenAI, 123 Pentonville Rd, London N1 9LG, UK

calendar.name: AI for the rest of us
calendar.description_short:
Shaping our future with AI. Creating opportunity through AI fluency, connection and community.

No jargon. No hype. No confusing terminology.

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Shaping our future with AI. Creating opportunity through AI fluency, connection and community.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "No jargon. No hype. No confusing terminology.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "NOTE: This event will be focussed on the changing shape of software development. Everyone is welcome to attend but the content will be somewhat technical.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Join us for our next in-person meet-up in London on Thursday, May 14th. ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "image", "attrs": {"alt": null, "src": "https://images.lumacdn.com/uploads/ka/c783f6c6-858c-47b4-b36e-7a343727ba68.png", "width": 2200, "height": 1200, "image_id": "local-upload-blob:https://luma.com/f637a155-8042-4b35-872b-7f44b9486d18", "mime_type": null, "uploaded_at": "2026-05-05T22:11:59.509+09:00", "is_uploading": false, "resize_width": null}}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Our theme for this meetup is ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Reimagining Software Development with AI.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " We're going to take a step back and think about the role we, the humans, play in the software development process now that coding agents have arrived.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This event provides a small glimpse of the future, from the innovators who are challenging everything and rebuilding the process of software development from the ground up, with humans at the centre!", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Where and When?", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Thursday, May 14th", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Doors open at 18:00", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Talks start at 19:00", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "AutogenAI, 123 Pentonville Rd, London N1 9LG", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Talk 1: The Validation Gap: We Need A Better Way To Review AI Generated Code", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " (Robert Werner, Co-Founder & CTO Leapter)", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "AI coding is fast. We're generating more code than ever. But more code means more code to verify, and agentic workflows are scaling that gap faster than review processes can keep up.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The bottleneck didn't disappear. It moved from writing code to verifying logic.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "For most generated code, you can ship, test, and iterate. But what if we need to be sure? What about the code that runs our financial systems or decides if you qualify for a loan? It has to be right.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hope is not a strategy, we need to better ways to review code. In this talk Robert will share his recent innovations, experiments and insights with you.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "About Robert:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Robert Werner is the CTO of an AI startup dedicated to shaping the future of AI-native software development. He has over 20 years experience focussed on Software Engineering, Developer Experience and Transformational Platform Engineering, across Fortune 500 companies and FinTechs.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Talk 2: Narrative Engineering: What Cognitive Science Actually Tells Us About Building with LLMs", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " (Sal Kimmich, Security Architect at Gadfly AI)", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We keep treating LLMs like search engines that hallucinate. That is the wrong category, and it produces the wrong engineering.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "LLMs are narrative generators. They do not retrieve facts or execute logic. They complete stories. That distinction is not philosophical. It determines what kind of system you can build with them and what kind you cannot. Neuroscience has been studying probabilistic, distributed, narrative-generating systems for decades. Most of the LLM engineering field is ignoring that work.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This talk walks through cyberneutics, an open source methodology and repository built on the premise that we need a new engineering discipline for narrative computing the way software engineering emerged from symbolic computing. The theoretical foundations come from second-order cybernetics, distributed cognition, and Minsky's Society of Mind. The empirical validation comes from 2025 mechanistic interpretability research showing that reasoning models internally simulate multi-agent dialogue to reason better. The practical techniques come from iterative practice: adversarial committees, pipeline algebra with formal quality propagation, observer-aware interaction design.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Three real lessons for practitioners building agentic systems: reliability is a property of the pipeline, not the prompt; repetition is latent space exploration, not failure; and if you want auditable AI reasoning, you need to externalise the dialogue structure, because the transcript is the explainability artifact.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The discipline does not exist yet. This is what building it looks like.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "About Sal: ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Sal is a developer advocate for open source and passionate about helping engineers, ethical hackers and digital enthusiasts understand the complexity of modern software development. With over a decade of experience as building cloud-native machine learning pipelines in the healthcare and tech for good sectors, their work is now focused on filling the cracks in the open source software supply chain to build a better digital future for all of us.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Thanks!", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "We'd like to thank our sponsors ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Leapter", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.leapter.com/"}}]}, {"text": " and ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "AutogenAI", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://autogenai.com/uk/"}}]}, {"text": " for making this event possible. ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Code of Conduct", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "This event has a ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "code of conduct", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://aifortherestofus.live/code-of-conduct"}}]}, {"text": ". By joining the community and registering for this event you agree to abide by our code of conduct.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Providing Your Name and Email", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "To register for this event you'll be asked to provide your email address. event.name: Ambassadors Club with Leanne Elliott Young
event.full_address: 12 Hay Hill, London W1J 8NR, UK

description: [{"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "Fashion is being rewritten in real time.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The world's biggest houses Prada, Balenciaga, Calvin Klein, H&M are scrambling to work out what identity, ownership and self-expression mean when bodies can be digital and clothes can live in code. The companies leading that conversation aren't the legacy ones. They're new institutes like IoDF.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "For this Ambassadors Club, we're sitting down with ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Leanne Elliott Young", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", co-founder and CEO of the Institute of Digital Fashion.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Under her leadership IoDF has delivered ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "seven industry world-firsts", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " in metaverse fashion and become the go-to partner for brands including Meta, Prada, Balenciaga, Calvin Klein, BMW, Selfridges, Soho House, Condé Nast, Vogue Italia, the Barbican and Frieze. She's been named one of the ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Global Top 100 Women of the Future in Emerging Tech", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", judges Vogue Business's Talent Competition, and has advised UK Trade on the economic potential of Web3.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "It's an intimate evening with drinks, canapés, and a proper conversation with one of the people actually building the future of one of the world's largest creative industries.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Expect:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Candid stories from inside global brand boardrooms", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hard-won lessons on selling innovation to a 200-year-old industry", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "A clear-eyed view of where fashion, AI and digital identity go next", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "And the room itself full of the top founders, operators and creatives from across the E2E community", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Looking forward to seeing you there.", "type": "text"}]}]

event.name: Learn to Work with Data in Claude (free, in-person training!)

hosts.name: Toby Mather
hosts.bio_short: Co-founder & CEO at Rig: use your internal data in Claude & Cursor

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Do you want to use AI tools like Claude more productively? In this 1hr live, hands-on event, you'll learn how to build a data-rich, on-brand slide deck with Claude. ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "If you're in a GTM, Finance, Ops or CS role and want to maximise the impact of your work with AI, come and learn how.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Being able to work with data, reliably, is a key skill to using AI tools at work. Come along to our live workshop to learn how to use Claude and MCPs/Connectors to work with data + AI.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Brought to you by the team behind Rig (", "type": "text"}, {"text": "https://rig.so", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://rig.so"}}]}, {"text": "), a context-aware data connector for AI tools.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We'll spend an hour doing hands-on work with a dataset we'll provide you access to on the day, and share our outputs, wins and fails at the end - then have a drink together!", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "All you need is a laptop and access to Claude Desktop (or Cursor, or Claude Code).", "type": "text"}]}]

How Design Teams Are Adopting AI", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Explore how design teams are integrating AI into everyday workflows, from research and ideation to prototyping and delivery, and what separates meaningful adoption from surface-level experimentation.", "type": "text"}]}]}]}]}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "2. event.name: She Bytes Back × Synthesia: The AI Workflows They Don't Post About

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "📅 Wednesday 20 May | 🕕 18:00 – 21:00 BST | 📍 ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Synthesia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.synthesia.io/"}}]}, {"text": "  HQ, London 🍕 Food & drinks on us | 🚨 Curated guest list with priority for senior software engineers", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "There's a polished version of \"how to use AI\" that lives on LinkedIn. Then there's the version senior engineers actually use, built through trial and error and shared quietly with teammates but never posted about.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This event is where that version gets shared openly.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "She Bytes Back are teaming up with Synthesia, one of the UK's most exciting AI-native product companies, to bring you an evening of real talk from senior women engineers who are using AI to change how they work.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Our speakers on the night:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "🎤 ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Hannah Craighead", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/hannah-craighead-a54222111/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " - Founding Engineer and employee #1 at ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Neurotype", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/company/neurotypeuk/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ", ex-", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Google", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/company/google/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ". She'll be breaking down how she ships features ridiculously fast using AI, and why the bottleneck was never code, it was clarity", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "🎤 ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Ornella Friggit-Konaté", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/ornellaf/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " - Staff Software Engineer at ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Bobsled", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/company/bobsled-inc/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " and fractional CTO consultant who joined Disney Streaming the day before Disney+ launched (yes, really). She's talking about how to use AI without burning through tokens or the planet.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "🎤 ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Catalina Oyaneder", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/cataflu/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " — Senior Software Engineer on ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Synthesia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/company/synthesia-technologies/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "'s Enterprise team. She's exploring what happens when AI removes the old excuses of \"too hard\" and \"too slow\" and why the craft isn't gone, it just moved up a level.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The agenda:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "6:00: Doors open for welcome drinks & food ", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "6:45: Kick off with Amber's introduction, housekeeping, and agenda + intro from Synthesia ", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "7:00 - 8:00: Talks on how senior engineers are using AI in their day to day", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "8:00-8:30: Networking games ", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "8:30-9:00: Closing networking", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This one's for you if:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "- You're a software engineer who's been building and shipping for a while", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "- You're curious about how senior engineers are integrating AI into their workflows", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "- You want to compare notes on how you've been using AI in your own work", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "- You want to connect with other senior women engineers in the community", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "- You're ready for conversations that go deeper than \"have you tried ChatGPT\"", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "And if you find yourself thinking \"wait, I want to work here\" during the evening, the Synthesia team will be around all night. Just saying 👀", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​", "type": "text"}, {"text": "About She Bytes Back", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "image", "attrs": {"alt": null, "src": "https://images.lumacdn.com/uploads/xc/7c14e851-3dff-40cf-a352-2af0c5d9ecf5.jpg", "width": 7008, "height": 4672, "image_id": "local-upload-blob:https://luma.com/c5450289-394b-4bdd-a650-a208b5190ba1", "mime_type": null, "uploaded_at": "2026-05-05T11:30:45.580+01:00", "is_uploading": false, "resize_width": null}}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​She Bytes Back is the UK's leading community for ambitious women in tech who are ready to stop being overlooked and start getting recognised for their expertise.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​With 3,000+ members across engineering, product, data, and leadership, we run sold-out events, partner with companies like Figma, Canva, and Depop, and help women build the visibility, connections, and confidence to accelerate their careers.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Founded by former software engineer turned keynote speaker Amber Shand, everything we do comes back to three things: find your voice, find your people, and make your expertise impossible to ignore.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "About Synthesia", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Synthesia is one of the UK's leading AI-native product companies, building the world's leading AI video communications platform. ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "They're also growing their engineering teams in London so feel free to explore their open roles on their careers page ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "HERE", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.synthesia.io/careers#open-positions"}}]}, {"text": ".", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "horizontal_rule"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "In the meantime, follow She Bytes Back on LinkedIn for updates, subscribe to the newsletter, and connect with Amber on LinkedIn.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Please familiarise yourself with our community's code of conduct, ensuring a safe and enjoyable experience for all attendees.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "By signing up to this event, you agree to the She Bytes Back privacy policy and are subscribed to the She Bytes Back mailing list. You can unsubscribe at any time.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "By RSVPing to this event, you consent to being recorded (photo and video) and agree that She Bytes Back and third parties, including event speakers, may use the recordings for promotional purposes, including sharing on social media and other platforms.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "By registering for this event, you agree that your name, email address, and relevant professional information may be shared with recruiters at Synthesia for the purpose of connecting you with potential career opportunities.", "type": "text"}]}]

featured_guests.name: jan lewis
featured_guests.bio_short: Freelance graphic designer, here to build your brand without creating unnecessary stuff or damaging the planet 😁

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This Get Sh*t Done event lives in the tension.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Not AI hype. Not AI panic. Just the reality of being in the media and creative industry right now. Between instinct and automation, craft and speed, taste and output.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This is for the people actually doing the work. The ones being asked to use AI, push it, question it — while still making something that doesn't feel generic.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hosted by Rebecca Rowntree, AI Creative Technologist, founder of Get Sh*t Done's sold-out events and 700+ creative community, and the person who built some of the internet's most talked-about AI personalities.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We're bringing together a CEO who helped scale one of the world's biggest podcasts, the founder of one of Europe's leading independent film festivals, and one of the most awarded female creative directors in the advertising industry.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "They're not here with a playbook. They're in it. Testing, breaking, rebuilding.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "And as always with Get Sh*t Done, this isn't about polished answers.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Bring your questions. Your doubts. Your half-formed ideas.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We'll talk honestly about what holds up, what falls apart and what still makes work worth making.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "horizontal_rule"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Georgie Holt | CEO, FlightStory", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Co-founder of FlightStory alongside Steven Bartlett, Georgie is the woman behind the platform that took ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "The Diary of a CEO", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}, {"text": " to become the 2nd biggest podcast globally in just five years and ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Begin Again", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}, {"text": " with Davina McCall. Named by the Hollywood Reporter as one of the most influential people in podcasting, she's now exploring what AI means for FlightStory's future.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Elliot Grove | Founder, Raindance Film Festival", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " The man who built Europe's largest independent film festival from scratch and has championed outsider filmmakers for over 30 years. Elliot is watching AI play out in real submissions, in real time. His perspective cuts through the noise: technology can change how films are made but it can't replace the instinct to tell a story that actually matters.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Laura Jordan Bambach | Founder & CCO, Uncharted", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " One of the most awarded female creative directors in the advertising industry, Laura walked away from the traditional agency model to build something new for the AI era with her agency Uncharted. Her take is grounded in practice. AI is powerful, but without a strong human perspective, it becomes \"blandification\". More optimism, less optimisation.", "type": "text"}]}]

event.name: Behind the Build: Inside Engineering at Ankar

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Behind the Build: Inside Engineering at Ankar", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Join us for an evening to meet the engineering team at ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Ankar", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://ankar.ai/"}}]}, {"text": ", featuring a fireside chat with ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Nick Denman", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.linkedin.com/in/nick-denman-6b0b35342/"}}]}, {"text": ", co-founder and Head of Engineering.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Nick leads the team building AI systems for one of the most complex and high-value knowledge domains in the world.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "At Ankar, the engineering team is building a suite of AI tools to help inventors explore, create, and protect ideas — starting with IP protection and expanding across the invention process. We've raised over $24M from Atomico, Index Ventures, Norrsken, Daphni, and angels from Hugging Face, Datadog, DeepMind, and OpenAI, and are already working with some of the world’s most innovative companies, including L’Oréal and Fenwick.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This fireside chat will go inside the Ankar engineering story: how the team thinks about building with AI, the technical challenges behind the product, what it takes to design systems for highly specialised knowledge work, and the kind of engineering culture we’re creating.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "After the chat, you'll also have the chance to meet engineers from across the Ankar team and hear firsthand how they’re approaching the hard technical and product problems behind our platform over food and drinks.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Ankar is actively hiring across product and engineering — so if you’re curious about what we’re building, come say hi ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "👀", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Space is limited. RSVP if you’d like to join us!", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "—", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Agenda", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "​6:30pm: Welcome", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "7:00pm: Fireside chat and Q&A", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "7:45pm: Meet the team, networking, food and drink", "type": "text"}]}]

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "A monthly coffee meet-up for designers, researchers and developers.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}]}, {"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "No agenda. No design patterns. No sense-making. Just caffeine (or decaf).", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Working in design or research can be a bit of a whirlwind. Whether you are currently deep in the Figma files, wrangling sticky notes that have lost their stick, or navigating the equally chaotic world of the job hunt, sometimes you just need to step away from the screen.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The Design Brewers", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " is a casual, monthly meet-up for anyone working in/with design. Each month, we pick a local coffee shop to explore the best brews while we chat, vent, or network (only if you fancy it).", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "The \"Agenda\"", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Show up whenever:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " We’ll be there for a couple of hours, so drop in when you can. No pressure to be there for the \"start\" or stay until the \"end\".", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Talk about anything:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Whether you want to moan about a tricky user flow, share a portfolio win, or just debate the best oat milk in town, the floor is yours.", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "All brews welcome:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Coffee, tea, decaf, or just a pastry. We pick shops that provide it all, so you actually have a choice (very human-centric, innit?).", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "Who is it for?", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "If your job involves solving problems for people, whether you’re a UX Designer, Product Designer, Service Designer, Researcher, Developer or anything in between, you’re a ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Design Brewer", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ".", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "If you are looking for your next role or pivoting careers into design, come and grab a seat. It’s a great place to hear what others are working on in a low-stakes environment.", "type": "text"}]}]

description: [{"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 1}, "content": [{"text": "A curated experience for crossing worlds.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "image", "attrs": {"alt": null, "src": "https://images.lumacdn.com/editor-images/bz/b8943825-fa9a-4134-8e12-740175576929.jpg", "width": 3090, "height": 2048, "image_id": "local-upload-blob:https://luma.com/77a952db-4b76-4a84-b9e3-6184ac70887d", "mime_type": null, "uploaded_at": "2026-04-21T17:42:42.648+01:00", "is_uploading": false, "resize_width": null}}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "find out what we've been up to ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "here", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://ripplesocialclub.com"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "horizontal_rule"}, {"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "​details.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Most people stay in one 'world'.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "This night is about crossing.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Confluence", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " brings together four distinct worlds ", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "- policy & impact, founder-builders, operators, and culture.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "You arrive in your world.", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "Then you move.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Across the room - event.name: The Fire Circle: London Walk
event.full_address: Colicci Ritz Corner, Green Park (Stop H, London W1J 9DZ, UK

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Looking for work is hard. Not just practically, emotionally. The uncertainty, the rejection, the feeling that you should always be on. It takes more out of you than most people admit.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Fire Circle walks are a day for you.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We’ll meet at 10am at the kiosk on Ritz Corner in Green Park and spend the morning walking north - through Regent’s Park, up to Primrose Hill, finishing at Hampstead Heath. The route takes us through some of London’s most beautiful green spaces, and that’s deliberate. Something shifts when you’re walking side by side with someone rather than sitting across from them. Conversations go deeper. You can connect without the pressure of a “meeting”. The movement helps too.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The format is a half-day walk - roughly 6 miles over 4–5 hours, with natural stops built in along the way. At each stop, Josh will open up a conversation around a theme - what you’re looking for, what you’ve learned, what’s actually going on in your search. You share as much or as little as you want. Between stops, walk with whoever you end up next to and see what comes up. That’s where the real value tends to be.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Josh, founder of Intrinsic Talent, will be there throughout. He’ll add structure to the day and is happy to offer a recruiter’s perspective on your search if you’d find that useful. But this isn’t a recruitment event. It’s a chance to spend a morning with people who understand what you’re going through, in a setting that reminds you there’s more to life than your inbox.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This is for tech professionals who are actively looking for work, recently made redundant, or figuring out what’s next. Come as you are.", "type": "text"}]}]

A fun evening for founders to switch off and get competitive.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Full Description:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Join us on", "type": "text"}, {"text": " from 5:00–6:30pm", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " for Geovation’s ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Games Night", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " a relaxed, after‑work social designed to help founders step away from pitch decks, product roadmaps and investor calls… and do something fun.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​We’ll be running ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "three different games", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " and rotating teams throughout the evening so you get to meet new people, team up with other founders, and enjoy some friendly competition.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Expect:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Three games, rotating every 20 mins", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​A chance to meet other Geovation founders in a fun, low‑pressure setting", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Snacks & drinks to fuel the competition", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​A space to reset and decompress after a long day of building", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "​Whether you're fiercely competitive or simply in it for the laughs, this night is for you. hosts.bio_short: Our mission is to build the sector for nature tech, scale nature-based decision making and close the nature-finance gap.

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Biodiversity loss and climate related risks are increasingly recognised as material considerations for the insurance sector. At the same time, new approaches to environmental data and modelling are emerging that make it possible to measure, monitor and verify nature-related change with greater consistency and transparency.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This event brings together participants from the insurance market, public sector and the nature tech community to explore how these developments can support risk assessment, underwriting and decision making.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The event will include a Keynote from Lloyd's of London followed by a nature tech innovation showcase, where selected companies present solutions to a panel of experts including DEFRA, actuaries, and industry leaders. Topics around nature tech, specifically environmental monitoring / modelling and insurance applications relevant to both climate and nature related risk.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "The event will be followed by a NatureTech Collective working group on Thursday the 14th of May at LSE, focused on data interoperability and the development of shared approaches for nature related data in insurance.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "PROGRAMME", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " ", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "17:00 – 18:00 Guest arrival & networking (Drinks and Canapés)", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "18:00 – 18:05 Welcome remarks", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "18:05 – 18:20 Keynote address (Luke Knowles, ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Head of Catastrophe Risk Analytics at Lloyd’s of London)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "18:20 – 18:40 Nature Tech Showcase", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Thomas Cromie, x10ai", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Donna Lyndsay, The True Position", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Beccy Wilebore, NatCap Research", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "18:40 - description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "From AI startups like Lovable and Wispr  to B2B SaaS companies and consumer tech brands, creators are becoming one of the most effective ways to drive awareness, trust, distribution AND conversions.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This workshop is a no-fluff introduction to how modern creator marketing actually works in 2026, especially for tech companies that have never tested it before.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We’ll cover:", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "• where creators fit across B2B and B2C funnels", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "• why “founder-led” and creator-led marketing are blending together", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "• how tech brands are using creators beyond simple paid ads", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "• what realistic budgets, KPIs and first tests look like", "type": "text"}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "• and how to approach creators strategically without wasting money", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This session will help you understand creator marketing from the basics to see if it’s worth investing for your company. event.name: NoBa Connect | The Future of Creative Talent & the Platforming of Representation

calendar.name: NoBa Connect
calendar.description_short: Where leaders, founders and investors connect to create the future of work.

description: [{"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 2}, "content": [{"text": "Unrepresented? Who Needs an Agent? - ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "The Future of Creative Talent & the Platforming of Representation", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "For decades, creative talent - from musicians and DJs to athletes and creators - has relied on agents and managers to access opportunity.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Not because they lacked talent, but because they lacked access.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Agents built relationships, opened doors, sourced work, and shaped careers in markets that were difficult to navigate alone.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "But that model is changing.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Many of the core functions of representation are now being delivered through platforms - giving individuals direct access to demand, tools, and distribution at a scale that wasn’t possible before. What was once relationship-driven and manual is becoming ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "structured, scalable, and increasingly self-serve", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ".", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "So what does it mean to be “represented” today?", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "At this NoBa Connect, we explore how representation is being redefined across ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "music, sport, and the creator economy", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " and whether talent still needs agents in the way it once did.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "blockquote", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "We’ll hear from founders from the NoBa portfolio and speakers working at the forefront of this shift - building platforms that enable talent to access opportunities, manage their careers, and capture more of the value they create.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Speaker announcements to follow soon.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "heading", "attrs": {"level": 1}, "content": [{"text": "Together, we’ll explore:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Why representation has historically been essential for creative talent", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "How platforms are replicating and reshaping those functions", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "What this means for individuals navigating their own careers", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Where human relationships still matter and where platforms win.", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "blockquote", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This isn’t the end of representation. But it may be the end of how we’ve always known it.", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}]}]

description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "An Evening with Sand River, ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": "Thursday 14th May 4pm-6pm, East London", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}, {"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Step into a little-known woodland in East London, for a magical evening of foraging, wild cocktails, folklore, and fire.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hosted by Sand River, this intimate gathering invites you to slow down and reconnect with nature as we explore the wild tastes, wild smells, wild stories and forgotten pathways of this special London enclave.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Guided by experienced forager Max Martin (@mindinnature), the evening will start with a short introduction before we walk through the woodland, discovering what’s in season and how it can be used. The journey ends at a fireside camp, where we’ll enjoy a cocktail or mocktail featuring wild ingredients - some of which you’ll have foraged yourself.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Sand River invests in early-stage startups that value, protect and restore nature. We like to host gatherings for people thinking deeply about how we work with the natural world.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "This event is suitable for anyone interested in the world of nature tech investing.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Practical details", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Date", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ": Thursday 14th May 2026 ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Time:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " 4:00pm – 6:00pm ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Meeting Point:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Meet at Mile End Tube Station ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "(exact woodland location will be revealed on the day)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "italic"}]}, {"text": " ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Dress:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Wear comfortable shoes; bring a layer in case it cools down ", "type": "text"}, {"text": "Dietary requirements", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": ": Cocktails and mocktails provided; dietary requirements and spirit preferences accommodated when you book. description: [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "About the Event", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Hardware is hard. Scaling it is even harder. London has the potential to unlock this.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Blue Garage, in collaboration with the Manufacturing Technology Centre, High Value Manufacturing Catapult, Innovate UK, and Made Smarter, is bringing together founders, engineers, and industry leaders to tackle a question that too few are asking directly:", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Why is it so difficult for hardware companies in London to scale, and what needs to change?", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Meet Founders, investors and business support services.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "What to expect:", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "A live pilot showcase featuring founder pitches and product demos, followed by honest, structured conversation about the challenges holding hardware companies back, and how the UK's leading organisations are positioned to help address them. You'll leave with a clearer picture of the pathways from early-stage idea to full-scale production, and direct connections to the people and programmes that can accelerate that journey.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Who You'll Meet", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Manufacturing Technology Centre (MTC)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.the-mtc.org/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " A leading UK research and technology organisation helping manufacturers innovate, develop, and scale. MTC brings advanced engineering expertise and world-class facilities to help turn ideas into commercially viable products.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "High Value Manufacturing Catapult (HVMC)", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://hvm.catapult.org.uk/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Bridging the gap between early-stage development and full-scale production. HVMC provides access to world-class manufacturing expertise, facilities, and technical support for high-potential hardware companies.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Innovate UK", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.ukri.org/councils/innovate-uk/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " ", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.ukri.org/councils/innovate-uk/"}}]}, {"text": "The UK's national innovation agency. Explore funding programmes, grants, and connections designed to help founders move from concept to commercial scale.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Made Smarter", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.madesmarter.uk/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " Supporting manufacturing SMEs to adopt new technologies and grow with confidence. Made Smarter offers tailored advice, funding, and digital transformation support to help businesses modernise and scale effectively.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Blue Garage", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "link", "attrs": {"href": "https://www.bluegarage.org/"}}, {"type": "bold"}]}, {"text": " An innovation hub in South East London where ideas can be designed, prototyped, and built under one roof. Members access labs, workshops, flexible studios, and the Blue Factory suite of prototyping and production machines, that all help founders take products from concept to reality.", "type": "text"}]}, {"type": "paragraph"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Agenda", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}]}, {"type": "bullet_list", "content": [{"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "13:00 Arrival & welcome ", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "13:45 Host introductions & open discussion", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "14:30 Break", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "14:45 Presentations & founder pitches ", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "15:15 Exhibition, networking & drinks", "type": "text"}]}]}, {"type": "list_item", "content": [{"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "17:00 Close", "type": "text"}]}]}]}, {"type": "paragraph"}, {"type": "paragraph", "content": [{"text": "Please note", "type": "text", "marks": [{"type": "bold"}]}, {"type": "hard_break"}, {"text": "By registering for this event, you consent to your details being shared with the event partners listed above: Manufacturing Technology Centre (MTC), High Value Manufacturing Catapult (HVMC), Made Smarter, Innovate UK, and Blue Garage.", "type": "text"}]}]
